tasks.title: Document blue-green rollback flow
tasks.content:
Rollback should reuse the same blue/green switch path as deployment. The previous color stays available until the new color is promoted and verified.

Rollback checklist:

- identify last known healthy color
- confirm old container is still present or restartable
- switch reverse proxy upstream back to the previous color
- verify health endpoint through the public proxy
- only then stop the failed color
tasks.portfolio_reason: Makes the zero-downtime Docker Compose story more credible by covering failure recovery.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm rollback depends on health checks, not manual guessing.

tasks.title: Add migration safety notes
tasks.content:
Zero-downtime deployment depends on backward-compatible database changes. A container switch cannot protect against destructive schema changes.

Safer migration pattern:

- expand schema first
- deploy app code that supports old and new schema
- backfill data if needed
- switch traffic after readiness checks
- remove old schema only after all app instances are upgraded
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows awareness of the boundary between deployment mechanics and application compatibility.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it warns about breaking migrations.

tasks.title: Define health endpoint contract
tasks.content:
Health checks should represent readiness, not only process liveness. The deployment script promotes a color only after the container reports ready.

A useful readiness endpoint should verify:

- application process is accepting requests
- critical dependencies are reachable
- migrations required by the running version are applied
- the app can serve a minimal real request path

files.content:
Health checks should represent readiness, not only process liveness. The deployment script promotes a color only after the container reports ready.

A useful readiness endpoint should verify:

- application process is accepting requests
- critical dependencies are reachable
- migrations required by the running version are applied
- the app can serve a minimal real request path

tasks.portfolio_reason: Strengthens the health-check-gated deployment claim with an explicit contract.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm the readiness criteria are concrete.

tasks.title: Document Nginx reload behavior
tasks.content:
The traffic switch relies on rendering a new upstream and reloading Nginx. Reloading should apply the new config while keeping the proxy process available.

Operational notes:

- validate generated config before production use
- reload rather than stop/start where possible
- keep the old color running during the switch
- restart only as a fallback path
tasks.portfolio_reason: Explains the reverse proxy behavior behind the Docker Compose zero-downtime pattern.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm it separates reload from restart fallback.

files.content:

Operational notes:

- validate generated config before production use
- reload rather than stop/start where possible
- keep the old color running during the switch
- restart only as a fallback path

tasks.title: Add deployment environment variable notes
tasks.content:
Deployment variables should be explicit so teams can reuse the template safely.

Important variables:

- `DEPLOY_HOST` and `DEPLOY_USER` for SSH target selection
- `DEPLOY_PATH` for server-side release location
- `APP_IMAGE` for the immutable image promoted to a color
- `STOP_OLD_AFTER_SWITCH` for keeping or stopping the old color
- `APP_PORT` for internal app readiness checks
tasks.portfolio_reason: Improves template usability for real GitLab CI deployment scenarios.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm variable names match repository conventions.

files.content:
Deployment variables should be explicit so teams can reuse the template safely.

Important variables:

- `DEPLOY_HOST` and `DEPLOY_USER` for SSH target selection
- `DEPLOY_PATH` for server-side release location
- `APP_IMAGE` for the immutable image promoted to a color
- `STOP_OLD_AFTER_SWITCH` for keeping or stopping the old color
- `APP_PORT` for internal app readiness checks

tasks.title: Add post-switch smoke test plan
tasks.content:
After switching traffic, a smoke test should verify the public path through the proxy, not only the container health state.

Minimum smoke tests:

- public HTTP endpoint returns success
- response identifies the expected app version or color when available
- critical API route responds within the expected timeout
- error rate remains stable after the switch
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows practical release validation beyond container orchestration.
tasks.test_instructions: Review the markdown file and confirm smoke tests are externally observable.

files.content:
After switching traffic, a smoke test should verify the public path through the proxy, not only the container health state.

Minimum smoke tests:

- public HTTP endpoint returns success
- response identifies the expected app version or color when available
- critical API route responds within the expected timeout
- error rate remains stable after the switch

tasks.title: Add immutable image tag policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Strengthens the deployment story by preventing mutable image tags in production releases.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm latest or empty tags are rejected.

files.content:
Zero-downtime deployment needs immutable images. A release should point to a versioned tag or digest so rollback and audit are predictable.

Policy checks:

- image value is present
- `latest` is not used
- digest or explicit version tag is present
- registry path is documented

tasks.title: Add rollback window guard
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows that old containers are retained long enough to make rollback practical.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm short rollback windows are rejected.

files.content:
A blue/green release should keep the previous color available until smoke checks and a minimum observation window pass. This turns rollback from an idea into an executable path.

Guard inputs:

- smoke test duration
- observation window
- stop-old-container flag
- minimum rollback window

tasks.title: Add compose profile policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Keeps the blue/green Compose layout explicit and reviewable.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm both color profiles are required.

files.content:
Compose profile policy should verify that both blue and green profiles exist and that shared infrastructure is not accidentally color-scoped. This keeps the deployment topology predictable.

Required profiles:

- blue
- green
- shared proxy service outside app color lifecycle

tasks.title: Add Nginx config validation plan
tasks.portfolio_reason: Makes proxy reload safety explicit before traffic is switched.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm reload is blocked when validation fails.

files.content:
Nginx configuration should be rendered and validated before reload. A failed validation must stop promotion and keep the previous color serving traffic.

Validation inputs:

- rendered config path
- validation command
- reload command
- rollback behavior

tasks.title: Add deployment lock policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows concurrent production deploys are serialized for predictable traffic switching.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm lock TTL must be positive.

files.content:
A deployment lock prevents two pipeline runs from switching colors at the same time. The lock should have an owner and TTL so abandoned deploys can be recovered safely.

Policy fields:

- lock name
- owner
- ttl seconds
- release behavior

tasks.title: Add pre-deploy disk check
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows the deploy flow verifies host capacity before starting a new color.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm low free disk space blocks deployment.

files.content:
Pre-deploy checks should verify enough free disk is available before pulling a new image or starting a new color. This prevents partial deployments caused by host capacity issues.

Checks:

- free disk percentage
- minimum free bytes
- image pull target filesystem
- fail-fast behavior

tasks.title: Add release metadata policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Makes releases auditable by requiring commit, image, and deployment color metadata.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm missing release metadata is reported.

files.content:
A zero-downtime deployment should record enough release metadata to explain what changed and how to roll it back. Metadata should travel with CI logs and release notes.

Required metadata:

- commit sha
- image reference
- target color
- deploy actor

tasks.title: Add deployment audit event policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows deployment events are auditable with actor, target, and result metadata.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm audit events require actor, commit, image, and result.

files.content:
Deployment audit events should capture who deployed what, where, and with what result. This keeps blue/green promotion traceable after incidents.

Required fields:

- actor
- commit sha
- image
- target color
- result

tasks.title: Add container startup budget policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows readiness waits are bounded and tuned to deployment expectations.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm startup attempts and intervals are bounded.

files.content:
Container startup budget should define how long the deployer waits before failing a color promotion. Bounded waits prevent stuck pipelines.

Policy fields:

- health attempts
- health interval seconds
- maximum startup budget
- failure action

tasks.title: Add compose health grace policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows blue/green promotion waits for readiness without hiding broken deployments.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm grace windows are bounded and positive.

files.content:
Compose health grace policy should define how long a new color is allowed to warm up before traffic promotion. The window must be long enough for startup but bounded for CI feedback.

Policy fields:

- warmup seconds
- health interval seconds
- maximum grace seconds
- failure action

tasks.title: Add rollback window policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows failed promotions keep the previous color available long enough for fast recovery.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm rollback windows preserve old color availability.

files.content:
Rollback window policy should keep the old color available after promotion until smoke checks and observation complete. This makes compose-based deploys safer under failure.

Policy fields:

- keep-old-color seconds
- smoke-check seconds
- minimum observation seconds
- cleanup allowed flag

tasks.title: Add smoke test manifest policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows zero-downtime promotion is gated by explicit post-start smoke checks.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm smoke manifests require named checks and timeouts.

files.content:
Smoke test manifest policy should make post-start validation explicit before promoting a new color. Each check should declare a name, endpoint, expected status, and timeout.

Policy checks:

- at least one smoke check
- every check has a name
- every check has a path
- timeout is positive

tasks.title: Add traffic shift plan policy
tasks.portfolio_reason: Shows compose promotion has controlled traffic movement and rollback points.
tasks.test_instructions: Run the unit test and confirm traffic shift steps are ordered and bounded.

files.content:
Traffic shift plan policy should keep blue/green promotion steps explicit. Traffic percentages must start at 0, finish at 100, and never move backwards.

Policy checks:

- starts at 0 percent
- ends at 100 percent
- percentages are ordered
- observation seconds are positive

tasks.title: Add deployment freeze window guard

files.content:
Production deployment automation should be able to respect freeze windows during incident response, high-traffic events, or maintenance restrictions. The guard should be explicit so the pipeline can fail early before starting containers or switching traffic.

Guard inputs:

- current UTC hour
- blocked deployment hours
- emergency override flag
- clear reason when deployment is blocked

tasks.portfolio_reason: Adds a realistic release-safety control for production deployment windows.
tasks.test_instructions: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` and confirm frozen hours block deployment unless override is explicit.

tasks.title: Add smoke result summary helper

files.content:
Smoke tests should leave a compact summary that can be attached to CI logs or release notes. A summary helps operators see which endpoint failed, how long checks took, and whether rollback should be considered.

Useful fields:

- check name
- target URL
- HTTP status
- duration in milliseconds
- pass/fail outcome

tasks.portfolio_reason: Improves release observability around smoke tests and rollback decisions.
tasks.test_instructions: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` and confirm smoke summaries mark slow or failing checks as failed.

tasks.title: Add deployment artifact integrity guard

files.content:
A blue-green release should promote a traceable image artifact, not an ambiguous mutable tag. The deployment plan can fail before traffic moves when the image digest, build identifier, or provenance marker is missing.

Integrity signals:

- image digest uses sha256
- build identifier is present
- source revision is recorded
- emergency bypass remains explicit

tasks.portfolio_reason: Adds a release-safety control that makes the Docker Compose deployment story more production credible.
tasks.test_instructions: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` and confirm mutable artifacts are rejected before promotion.

tasks.title: Add rollback readiness check

files.content: A zero-downtime rollback is only possible when the previous color is still healthy and the proxy can switch back safely. This check turns that operational assumption into an explicit release gate.

tasks.portfolio_reason: Makes rollback claims concrete by validating health and proxy readiness before release traffic moves.
tasks.test_instructions: Run `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` and confirm missing rollback gates are visible.
